summary: Full backend + frontend testing of camera-hub-personal. 23/23 pytest backend tests pass (system status, cameras CRUD, snapshots live & 503 offline cloud, PTZ rotatable demo & 400 indoor, recordings, motion events seed/simulate/ack/snapshot/delete, settings GET+PUT persistence, tuya status & 400 unconfigured, and WebSocket /api/ws proxy receiving >100KB of fMP4 binary + text negotiation). Frontend dashboard renders the 'SURVEILLANCE-VIDÉO' header, '3/8 en ligne' counter, camera-grid with 8 camera-feed tiles; the 3 live tiles show JPEG snapshots (test patterns) with PTZ/RTSP/DUAL badges, cloud tiles display 'CLOUD — EN VEILLE' with SMARTLIFE badge. Fullscreen modal opens with ptz-controller and all ptz-control-{up,down,left,right,home} buttons; record-button creates a clip (toast 'Clip enregistré'). Sidebar tabs (cameras/events/system) all functional: add-camera form creates a camera, simulate-motion-button produces an event with thumbnail, system tab shows go2rtc EN LIGNE / version 1.9.14 / 8 cams. Settings dialog opens, player-mode and tuya switches present, save returns 'Réglages enregistrés' toast; tuya/test 400 confirmed via console.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:434 — WebSocket proxy: when upstream connect fails (websockets.connect raises), the client receives only a close after the inner try/except logs a warning. Consider sending a structured close code / text to help client surface a friendly error.
server.py:432 — @app.websocket('/api/ws') is mounted on the app (not the api router). Works because the path is '/api/ws', but inconsistent with the rest of the routes. Acceptable but worth noting.
server.py:534 — startup sync_camera_stream loop swallows individual stream errors only as a whole; one bad RTSP could mask others. Consider per-camera try/except.

Frontend SettingsDialog: 'settings-tuya-test' button is only rendered when form.tuya.enabled is true (expected). The toggle uses Radix Switch — tests must click and wait for re-render before asserting visibility of the conditional sub-fields. Not a bug.
Live tiles render a JPEG snapshot overlay because the sandbox Chromium cannot decode H.264 (confirmed limitation per request). No code change needed for sandbox; real browsers will play MSE video.
seed_data_creation: Created and then deleted: TEST_Cam_RTSP (API CRUD) and TEST_UI_Cam (UI add-camera). Toggled settings.tuya.enabled and reset back to false at end of run. Settings player_mode reset to 'mse', motion_detection reset to true.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend lives at https://freebox-hub-stream.preview.emergentagent.com (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). 8 demo cameras are seeded automatically by server.py on first start (only if cameras collection is empty); to re-seed wipe collections cameras/motion_events/recordings then restart backend. go2rtc supervisor program is 'go2rtc' (do not restart mongodb). The 3 'demo_*' streams are synthetic ffmpeg mires defined in /app/go2rtc/go2rtc.yaml — they are H.264, so Playwright headless cannot decode the <video> element; verify live status via (a) /api/cameras/{id}/snapshot JPEG bytes and (b) /api/ws receiving binary fMP4 (see TestWebSocket.test_ws_proxy_demo_entrance). The 'settings-tuya-test' button is conditionally rendered when tuya.enabled=true; tests must enable the Radix Switch first and wait ~500ms for the conditional fields to mount.
rca of the issue: No defects found. All backend endpoints behave as specified and all frontend UI flows complete successfully.
